summary: Epic Playin’ > Drum jam > Wharf Rat with fiery Throwing Stones
blurb: The second set erupts with a monster 17‑minute Playing In The Band → Drums → Wharf Rat arc and a scorching Throwing Stones. Jerry’s crisp guitar and Vince’s clean keys sit on top of a tight rhythm foundation. First set is uneven but the show peaks in the latter half.
review: On July 19, 1994 at Deer Creek Music Center the Dead delivered one of those classic late‑’90s second‑set blowouts. After a funky Shakedown Street opener that quickly gave way to a middling first set (Broken Arrow and Easy Answers sag), the band launched into a soaring sequence: Foolish Heart segues into Saint Of Circumstance, then into I Want To Tell You before exploding into an epic Playing In The Band → Drums → Wharf Rat jam that stretches beyond 17 minutes. The improvisational intensity is driven by Phil’s melodic bass lines and Jerry’s inventive, crystal‑clear guitar tone, while Mickey and Bill lay down a powerful double‑drum groove that reviewers described as “crisp” and “ambient with insect texture.” The momentum carries straight into a fiery Throwing Stones that showcases Vince’s balanced keyboards and Bob Weir’s solid rhythm work. A brief Not Fade Away wraps the set, and the encore Rain provides a gentle close. Across three soundboard recordings the mix is praised as pristine—digital SBD captures every nuance, from Jerry’s grizzled vocals on Wharf Rat to the precise percussion on the Drumz segment. While a few reviewers note lyrical slips on Foolish Heart and occasional vocal fatigue, the overall performance is strong enough to recommend this show for anyone chasing that mid‑’90s jam energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Mixed sentiment dominates (2 mixed, 1 positive, 1 negative), but praise for the epic Playing In The Band > Drum > Wharf Rat jam and Throwing Stones outweighs first‑set criticisms, yielding a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent digital soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, full show length, and no dropouts – the definitive listening source.

key_highlights: Monster 17‑minute Playing In The Band → Drums → Wharf Rat jam
Scorching Throwing Stones that drives the second set peak
Strong opener with Shakedown Street and tight rhythm section
Vince’s clean, well‑balanced keyboards complement Jerry’s crisp guitar

band_performance.Jerry: Inventive, crisp guitar tone shines on the jam; vocals are grizzled and add character, though a few lyric slips and moments of sounding "out of it" were noted.
band_performance.Phil: Provides melodic bass leads that drive the intensity of the Playing In The Band segment; highlighted as a top performer in the jam.
band_performance.Bob: Outstanding rhythm guitar throughout, keeping the groove solid during both sets.
band_performance.Vince: Generally competent with clear, well‑balanced playing; no standout solos but adds essential texture to the overall sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful double‑drum work; crisp and audible on Throwing Stones and throughout the Drumz segment.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey delivers a tight, precise percussion foundation that fuels the epic second‑set jams.
